title: Complex Creamy Slovakian Fish Tagine
description: A sour and dense pescatarian-friendly Slovakian dish featuring parma ham, served in a traditional clay tajine vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Blanch the cod fillets in boiling water for 2 minutes, then set aside.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the parma ham to the skillet and cook until crispy, then set aside.
In the same skillet, add the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the chicken broth, lemon juice, and heavy cream, and bring to a simmer.
Add the blanched cod fillets to the skillet and spoon some of the sauce over the top of each fillet.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 12 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
Garnish with parsley and crispy parma ham, and serve in a clay tajine vessel.
